preamble: The complete elision of the velar stop [k] is the final stage of a lenition chain that typically moves from stop to fricative to glide before disappearing entirely.

examples.note: Indonesian nasal prefix meN-: initial /k/ is deleted and replaced by the nasal /ŋ/ of the prefix (KPST rule: k, p, s, t deleted after nasal prefix).
Complete elision of the intervocalic velar stop.
Loss of the final velar consonant over time.
